message:
viz: extract CSS to frankentui.css and refactor HTML from 16.6K to 464 lines

Major architectural refactor of the spec evolution visualization,
extracting all styling into a standalone CSS file and dramatically
slimming the monolithic HTML.

frankentui.css (157 lines, new):
  - Standalone stylesheet implementing the FrankenTUI dark terminal
    aesthetic: phosphor-green (#33ff00) on near-black (#050505),
    purple accent (#bd00ff), error red (#ff003c)
  - CSS custom properties for consistent theming across all components
  - JetBrains Mono as the primary monospace font
  - Base layout: full-viewport flexbox with sidebar + content area
  - Reusable component styles: panel headers, scrollable regions,
    stat grids, toggle buttons, progress bars, diff viewer, tooltips
  - Responsive design foundations and dark-mode-first approach
  - CRT glow effects via box-shadow on key UI elements

visualization HTML (-16,147 / +448 lines, net -15,699):
  - Reduced from ~16,600 lines to 464 lines by extracting:
    (a) all CSS to frankentui.css (linked at line 22)
    (b) leveraging the previously-committed SQLite dataset pipeline
        (sql.js WASM loads spec_evolution_v1.sqlite3 at runtime)
  - Page-specific overrides remain in an inline <style> block
    (sidebar layout, content area, pane headers, commit list items)
  - External dependencies loaded from CDN: JetBrains Mono font,
    highlight.js for syntax highlighting, diff2html for diff rendering
  - Clean separation of concerns: CSS theming is now reusable across
    any FrankenSQLite web tooling, not locked inside one HTML file

The deployed Cloudflare Pages site loads identically — same charts,
same interactions, same data — but the source is now maintainable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
